text:
Princess Anne’s daughter Zara Tindall’s dreams of returning to the Olympics have been dashed after it was confirmed the royal equestrian has been left off the United Kingdom’s Paris squad.

Royal family member Zara Tindall will not be representing Team Great Britain at the 2024 Paris Olympics this summer, it has been confirmed.

The only daughter of Princess Anne and Captain Mark Phillips won a silver medal in Team Eventing at the 2012 London Olympics and is one of the country’s leading equestrian riders.

Tindall, 43, confirmed to Vogue in 2022 that she was back in training in the hopes of earning a spot in the Paris squad and taking another run at a gold medal.

However, the British Olympics committee have confirmed the eventing team representing Great Britain in the French capital will be Rosalind Canter, Laura Collett, Tom McEwen, and Yasmin Ingham.

The decision marks the fifth non-consecutive Olympics where Tindall has been left off the Team GB squad.

Tindall missed the 2004 Athens and 2008 Beijing games because of injuries to her horse.

In 2016, Tindall technically qualified for the 2016 Rio Olympics but was left off the final squad due to more concerns over her horse’s fitness.

The eldest granddaughter of Queen Elizabeth II opted out of qualifying for the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, which occurred around the time of the birth of her third child.

Both of Zara parents were also pro-equestrians and Olympians but Tindall was the first ever British royal to win a medal.

Anne competed in the 1976 Montreal Olympics as an equestrian but missed out on a medal after falling off her horse Goodwill and cracking a vertebrae.

Meanwhile, Zara’s dad Captain Phillips won two gold medals at the 1972 Munich Games and 1988 Seoul Games in Team Eventing.

The update comes just weeks afteran unnamed source inside Buckingham Palace revealed the Tindalls were the "glue of their generation of royals" following Kate and Charles’s cancer diagnoses.

Zara and her husband Mike, 45, a retired rugby great, are fiercely loyal to the monarchy and have reportedly cut ties with Prince Harry and Meghan Markle for betraying Charles.

"They can help take the family's mind off the gravity of what is going on," the source told The Times newspaper.

"While some family members can be very "royal", Mike is just himself and is relaxed and playful with George, Charlotte, and Louis.

"It helps that they all have children around the same age that they can fool around together privately and bring a sense of normality to what is a stressful situation for everyone involved."
